mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has been pinned near zero (0.08% vintage value) since the pandemic shock, mechanically compressing the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting elevated equity valuations. Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels are associated with positive forward S&P 500 returns as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market healing during the post-pandemic recovery, which raises expected consumer income and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher once UNRATE falls by at least 0.3 percentage points over two consecutive readings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >= 0.3pp over two consecutive monthly releases
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage_value=6.2 as of 2021-02-01, unchanged from prior revision
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE decline >= 0.3pp fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect reverses sign in a rolling 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand recovery. Because these two series capture complementary but distinct demand channels, a synchronized upturn in both is a stronger signal of durable economic expansion than either series alone, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 1% AND HOUST 3-month growth > 5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=559230 vs vintage 568215 as of 2021-01-01
fact:HOUST revised_value=1630 vs vintage 1580 as of 2021-01-01
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fiscal stimulus support
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows 60-day forward return win rate not statistically distinguishable from the single-series RSAFS-only or HOUST-only trigger samples (chi-square p > 0.10), indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rebounding from pandemic-induced trough levels signals restoration of manufacturing capacity utilization, which in a post-shock recovery regime disproportionately boosts cyclical equity earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 returns higher over a full quarter, but this relationship is regime-specific and does not hold in mature expansions where supply is already at capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=98.8822 vs vintage 107.1843 as of 2021-01-01 indicating large downward revision from pandemic trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO month-over-month gains are not significantly positive during 2020-2022 recovery window (p > 0.05), or the same signal produces no excess return in a mid-cycle regime placebo test, falsifying regime specificity.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in headline CPI above a threshold pace signals rising inflation risk that raises market expectations of an earlier Federal Reserve tightening cycle, compressing near-term equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors reprice rate-hike odds.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=263.579 vs vintage 263.161 as of 2021-02-01, showing upward revision
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI prints exceeding the 3.5% annualized threshold are not negative on average, or show no statistically significant difference from unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05) across a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value=143380 vs vintage 143048 as of 2021-02-01, confirming upward payroll revision trend
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns conditioned on positive 3-month PAYEMS trend fail to outperform unconditional baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp surge in housing starts above a defined threshold indicates strong residential investment momentum, which historically leads broader economic activity and corporate earnings growth by one to two quarters, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter once HOUST crosses an expansionary threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=1630 as of 2021-01-01, above the 1,600k threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST crossing above 1,600k units fail to exceed the unconditional 60-day return distribution median in more than half of historical crossing events since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: The joint combination of near-zero FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL implies a deeply negative real policy rate, which incentivizes short-term risk-taking and equity flows because cash and bonds offer negative real yield; this interaction effect predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the very short term, distinct from either series acting alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value=0.08 and CPIAUCSL revised_value=263.579 as of Feb 2021, implying negative real rate
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during periods satisfying both the low-FEDFUNDS and rising-CPI conditions jointly do not differ significantly from returns during periods when only one condition holds (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10 in regression test).
